description: False positive tracking for security scans. Findings listed here have been manually reviewed and dismissed.

B608.reason: All SQL queries use parameterized ? placeholders or f-string table names with regex validation

B310.reason: Intentional URL opens in CLI single_agent mode — not user-controlled input

B108.reason: Temp file/directory usage in sandbox verifier and test code — acceptable risk

B314.reason: defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() called at module load in scanner_parsers.py — all XML parsing is safe

SAST-024.reason: Admin endpoints have auth enforced at router mount level via Depends(_verify_api_key, _require_scope('admin:all')) in app.py:990-994

SAST-007.reason: Pattern strings in SAST rule detection lists (e.g., 'pickle.loads(' in autofix_engine.py:916), not actual deserialization calls

SAST-077.reason: Pattern strings in SAST rule detection lists (e.g., 'eval(' in autofix_engine.py:906), not actual eval usage

SAST-089.reason: Code actually BLOCKS cloud metadata endpoints (workflows_router.py:290 — blocked set includes 169.254.169.254). DAST engine references are for testing.

SAST-028.reason: Configurable verify_ssl for pen-testing tools (micro_pentest.py:190). Self-signed cert support is required for MPTE.

SAST-065.reason: Pattern string 'yaml.load(' in SAST rule detection list, not actual yaml.load call

SAST-081.reason: Pattern string 'pickle.loads(' in SAST rule detection list, not actual pickle usage

SAST-001.reason: PersistentDict table name validation added (regex [a-zA-Z0-9_]). All queries parameterized.

SAST-091.reason: Scanner engines intentionally test various URL schemes (file://, ftp://) — this is their purpose

SAST-006.reason: Enum values (API_KEY='api_key', PASSWORD='password') — not actual credentials

SAST-027.reason: SAST rule detection patterns for ECB mode, not actual ECB usage

graph_py_format_token.reason: Git log format string, not a secret token

scanner_dashboard_example.reason: Example code shown in UI demo for scanner demonstration
